message:
Migrate from Poetry to uv, replace black+isort with ruff (#25)

* Migrate from Poetry to uv, replace black+isort with ruff

- Convert pyproject.toml files to PEP 621 format with hatchling build backend
- Replace poetry.lock with uv.lock in both backend and CLI packages
- Update all Dockerfiles to use uv instead of Poetry
- Update GitHub Actions workflows to use astral-sh/setup-uv@v5
- Replace black and isort with ruff for linting and formatting
- Update documentation (README.md, CLAUDE.md) with new commands

Command mapping:
- poetry install -> uv sync
- poetry run <cmd> -> uv run <cmd>
- poetry run black . -> uv run ruff format .
- poetry run isort . -> uv run ruff check --fix .

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Apply ruff auto-fixes for import sorting and code style

Auto-fixed issues include:
- I001: Import block sorting
- UP: Python upgrade suggestions (modern syntax)
- SIM: Simplification suggestions
- F: Pyflakes errors (unused imports, etc.)

Remaining issues to address separately:
- E501: Line too long (455 in backend, 45 in CLI)
- B: Bugbear issues requiring manual review

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Configure ruff with appropriate ignores for existing codebase

- Increase line-length to 100
- Add ignores for patterns that need careful review (B904, E722, etc.)
- Add ignores for existing patterns like star imports (F403, F405)
- Add per-file ignore for re-exports in __init__.py
- Fix circular import in platform_api/infra/__init__.py with isort:skip_file

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix CI workflow issues after uv migration

- Replace black check job with ruff in lint.yaml
- Add --group cli to uv sync commands where invoke is needed:
  - backend-local.yaml
  - backend-tests.yaml (2 jobs)
  - deploy.yaml (2 jobs)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Apply ruff formatting to backend

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix serverless deployment after uv migration

- Set usePoetry: false in config-common.yaml since we now use uv
- Add requirements.txt generated from uv lock for serverless-python-requirements

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Enable slim mode for serverless-python-requirements

Reduce Lambda package size by removing unnecessary files like
__pycache__, .pyc, .dist-info, and tests directories.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Regenerate requirements.txt without hashes

The hashes were causing issues with serverless-python-requirements plugin.
Also explicitly set strip: false to avoid breaking native extensions.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix Lambda size limit and workflow dependency groups

- Use Lambda Layer for dependencies to stay under 250MB limit
  (AWS best practice: bundle boto3 for version stability)
- Add --all-groups to test workflows that need pytest/invoke
- Add uv sync --group dev to lint workflow for ruff
- Update docs to recommend --all-groups for local development

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Exclude boto3 from Lambda package to stay under size limit

Lambda Layer alone wasn't enough - the layer also has a 250MB limit.
Adding noDeploy for boto3/botocore saves ~100MB. This is safe because:
- Lambda runtime includes boto3/botocore
- Our code uses standard boto3 APIs (client, resource, Session)
- No version-specific features are required

Also added more slim patterns to further reduce size.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove layer, enable strip, add more aggressive slim patterns

- Removed layer: true (still hitting 250MB limit)
- Enabled strip: true to reduce native binary sizes
- Added more slim patterns (*.c, *.h, *.pyx, doc/, sample/)
- Added urllib3 to noDeploy (provided by Lambda runtime)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove boto3/botocore from requirements.txt directly

The noDeploy config wasn't working due to cache issues.
Removing boto3 and its dependencies directly from requirements.txt
invalidates the cache and reduces package size by ~100MB.

boto3/botocore are provided by the Lambda runtime.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Reduce Lambda package size by removing unused deps

- Remove unused psycopg (v3 async) - only psycopg2 is used
- Move docker to dev dependencies - only used for local development
- Make DockerInfra import lazy to avoid loading docker package in Lambda
- Regenerate requirements.txt cleanly (was corrupted from manual editing)
- Add offline size measurement script for testing without CI

Net Lambda package size: ~74 MB (well under 250 MB limit)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Disable serverless-python-requirements cache

Force fresh pip install to ensure noDeploy exclusions are applied.
Previous builds may have cached packages including boto3.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move boto3 to dev dependencies - Lambda runtime provides it

Since serverless-python-requirements noDeploy option doesn't work,
remove boto3 from main dependencies entirely. Lambda runtime includes
boto3, so it will be available at runtime. For local development,
use `uv sync --group dev`.

This should reduce Lambda package size by ~27 MB (boto3 + botocore).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Make boto3 imports lazy to fix Docker compose tests

boto3 is now a dev dependency (Lambda runtime provides it), but the
code was importing it at module load time. This caused import errors
in Docker containers where boto3 isn't installed.

Changes:
- stores/s3.py: Import boto3/botocore inside __init__ methods
- infra/__init__.py: Use lazy __getattr__ for AwsInfra (like DockerInfra)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Include docker in runtime deps

Move docker from dev to runtime dependencies so compose tests can run.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add boto3 to runtime dependencies

The lazy import workaround was incomplete - admin.py directly imports
from infra/aws.py, which requires boto3 at module load time. Adding
boto3 to runtime deps ensures compose tests can run.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-# Dockerfile setup inspired by
-# https://dev.to/farcellier/package-a-poetry-project-in-a-docker-container-for-production-3b4m

 # NOTE: In order to be useful, the `$XDG_CACHE_HOME/tenzir-platform` directory
 # (usually `~/.cache/tenzir-platform`) needs to be mapped to an external volume,
 # to be able to persist the login information between runs.
 
 FROM python:3.11-slim AS builder
 
-RUN pip install poetry
+# Install uv
+COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /uvx /bin/
+
 RUN mkdir -p /app
 COPY . /app
 
 WORKDIR /app
-RUN poetry config virtualenvs.in-project true --local
-RUN poetry install --without dev
+RUN uv sync --frozen --no-dev --no-group types
 
 FROM python:3.11-slim AS base
